title: Primary Biliary Cholangitis
chief_complaint: I've been itching all over my body for 6 months, especially at night. I'm exhausted all the time. I also have dry eyes and dry mouth.

physical_exam.Skin: Excoriations from scratching. Xanthelasma around eyes. No jaundice.
physical_exam.Abdomen: Mild hepatomegaly. No splenomegaly. No ascites.

hidden_data.lft: ALP 520 (markedly elevated), GGT 380, ALT 95, AST 85, Total Bilirubin 0.8
hidden_data.ama: Anti-Mitochondrial Antibody (AMA): POSITIVE (1:640 titer). Diagnostic for PBC.
hidden_data.us_abdomen: Normal liver echotexture. No biliary dilation. No stones. Normal gallbladder.
hidden_data.liver_biopsy: Florid bile duct lesion with lymphocytic infiltration and non-caseating granulomas. Bile duct destruction. Stage 2 fibrosis (periportal fibrosis).

correct_diagnosis: Primary Biliary Cholangitis (PBC) - AMA Positive, Stage 2
key_learning_points: PBC = autoimmune destruction of intrahepatic bile ducts. 90% women, age 40-60. Associated with Sjögren syndrome (dry eyes/mouth)
Diagnosis requires 2/3: Elevated ALP, Positive AMA, Liver biopsy showing florid bile duct lesion. AMA is diagnostic (positive in 95%)
Symptoms: Fatigue (most common, 70%), Pruritus (intense, worse at night), Sicca symptoms (Sjögren overlap). Hyperlipidemia with xanthelasma
Treatment: Ursodeoxycholic acid (UDCA) 13-15mg/kg/day - first-line. Slows disease progression. Obeticholic acid (OCA) for UDCA-inadequate responders. Cholestyramine for pruritus
Monitor: ALP (response to UDCA predicts survival), bilirubin (rising = poor prognosis). Screen for HCC if cirrhosis. Osteoporosis prevention (vitamin D + calcium)
